title: jovibarba hirta subsp. neilreichii
intro: Jovibarba hirta subsp. neilreichii, also known as the hairy rock-jovibarba, is a rare and unique subspecies of the Jovibarba hirta plant, which is a type of low-growing, rhizomatous succulent native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the hairy rock-jovibarba has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective effects. In herbal preparations, the hairy rock-jovibarba is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
